zh.Tene & Hopton, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾霍普頓
zh.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene是Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene和*E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE的兒子，於1120年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾霍普頓。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1166年在英國英格蘭享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene是Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene和*E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE的儿子，于1120年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾霍普頓。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1166年在英国英格兰享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Tene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Cheng, Chia, Chia-Chi, Chunyan, Kam, Ming, Ming Yi, Wei, Yu Ming, Yung, Beng, Bin.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 滕 (or rarely 腾), meaning ‘(water) surging’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Teng (滕), the name of a state (located in present-day Tengxian in Shandong province), which was given to Cuo Shu Xiu, the 14th son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ).(ii) said to be borne by one of the 25 sons of the legendary emperor Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (iii) adopted as a Han Chinese surname by minority ethnic groups such as Manchus and Mongolians.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form (also Teochew or Hokkien form) of the surname 鄧, see   Deng  .

variants.name: Deng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Hua, Jian, Jing, Wei, Wen, Yi, Ying, Chao, Fang, Gang, Hui, Min.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鄧: (i) from Deng (鄧), the name of a state (located in Dengxian in Henan province) granted to Man Ji, uncle of Wu Ding (a king of the Shang dynasty (1600 –1046 BC )). (ii) from Deng Bowen (鄧伯溫), head of an ancient state (located in present-day Shandong province) dating back to the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (iii) from Deng (鄧), a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to descendants of Zhong Kang, the 4th king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600 BC ). (iv) from Deng Cheng (鄧城, City of Deng), a town in the state of Cai (located in present-day Henan province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). (v) from Deng (鄧), a title of Li Tianyi, the 8th son of Li Yu (the last king of the state of Southern Tang (937–975 AD ) during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms (907–960) periods). The state of Southern Tang was annexed by Emperor Taizong of Song (939–997 AD ) in 975 AD , and the royal families of South Tang were hunted down. Li Tianyi's son fled and changed the surname (Li) to Deng (鄧), his father's title.

nameMeanings.name: -Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Cheng, Chia, Chia-Chi, Chunyan, Kam, Ming, Ming Yi, Wei, Yu Ming, Yung, Beng, Bin.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 滕 (or rarely 腾), meaning ‘(water) surging’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Teng (滕), the name of a state (located in present-day Tengxian in Shandong province), which was given to Cuo Shu Xiu, the 14th son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ).(ii) said to be borne by one of the 25 sons of the legendary emperor Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (iii) from Deng (鄧), a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to descendants of Zhong Kang, the 4th king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600 BC ). Li Tianyi's son fled and changed the surname (Li) to Deng (鄧), his father's title.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene was born in 1120, in Hopton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene and *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1166, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene est né en 1120, à Hopton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene et *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1166, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene wurde 1120 in Hopton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene und *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1166 im Alter von 46 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene è nato il nel 1120 nel Hopton, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene e *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1166, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene(은)는 1120년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 홉턴에서 Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene(와)과 *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1166년 영국 잉글랜드에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene nasceu em 1120, em Hopton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene e *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1166, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene родился в 1120 г. в местности хоптон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene и *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1166 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William Tene -Lord of Tene nació en 1120, en Hopton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Ilbert fitzGilbert de Tene y *E MRS Ilbert FitzGILBERT de TENE. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1166, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años.
